title: senecio orthophyllus
intro: Senecio orthophyllus, also known as the annual burnet, is a plant species within the daisy family (Asteraceae) characterized by its annual or biennial growth habit and the presence of daisy-like flowers. The plant contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties and potential health benefits. Senecio orthophyllus has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and the risk of liver damage.
